text.title: integration-testing Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure: pin image -> session fixture -> tx fixture -> DI override -> CI wiring.

step.name: pick-and-pin
step.action: Choose Testcontainers modules; pin to a specific patch version per service.
step.decision-gate: If a service has no Testcontainers module, use CI-native service block (github-actions services).

step.name: session-container
step.action: Add session-scoped fixture in tests/integration/conftest.py creating one container + engine + schema for the whole pytest session.
step.decision-gate: If schema migrations are slow, cache the migrated container via Testcontainers reuse or volume snapshot.

step.name: transaction-rollback-session
step.action: Add function-scoped fixture that opens a connection, BEGIN, yields a Session, then ROLLBACK on teardown — even on test failure.
step.output: db_session fixture using yield + finally tx.rollback().
step.decision-gate: If using async SQLAlchemy 2.x, use AsyncSession + connection.begin() context manager.

step.name: di-override
step.action: Override the framework's DB dependency to yield the test session; ensure clean-up clears dependency_overrides after the test.
step.decision-gate: If your framework lacks a DI hook, use middleware to swap the session — but DI is preferred.

step.name: ci-wire
step.action: Add a workflow step that installs Docker, runs `pytest tests/integration -v --maxfail=1`. Track suite time in PR comment.
step.decision-gate: If suite > 5 minutes, split by path or parallelize via pytest-xdist + per-worker DB schema.
